events.note: Starting scope — pushing intent check for coverage expansion and data gap fixes

events.change_summary: Initial intent check — expand coverage to top 50 countries, address data gaps

events.summary: Intent check pushed — expands coverage to top 50 countries and addresses output data gaps. Flags ambiguity about which specific data gaps to tackle.

events.note: Writing full scope (revision 2) for coverage top-50 + output data gap fills

events.change_summary: Full scope: expand coverage to top 50 countries; fill identified output data gaps (Radar overlay for uncovered/remote-only ISPs; resilient partial baseline speedtest)

events.rationale: Primarily fills incomplete/broken output behavior (blank ISP rows, discarded partial baseline) plus a one-line coverage default change. No new web UI — all output is existing Rich terminal patterns, so skip design. Well-contained to cli.py/display.py/speedtest.py building on existing patterns (reuses _fetch_rum, probe_errors convention), no new subsystems or system boundaries, so skip architecture. Detailed phases with Files/Verification written in-scope.

events.decision: Interpret "cover all the gaps" as the two concrete, observable gaps in the pasted country US run — (1) blank "no coverage"/"remote-only" ISP rows that never fetch ASN-level Cloudflare Radar data because they short-circuit before _measure(), and (2) speedtest.run() discarding a successful direction when the other fails. Backfill Radar via the existing _fetch_rum path rather than routing skipped ASNs through _measure() (which would trigger unwanted trace probes toward dead targets). Reverse-DNS hop enrichment and per-ISP throughput are explicitly deferred and flagged as an open question for the builder.
events.rationale: "Cover all the gaps" is open-ended; committing to the observable gaps keeps the task bounded and verifiable while surfacing the ambiguity to the builder instead of silently guessing scope. Reusing _fetch_rum avoids side effects and matches existing patterns.

events.summary: Full scope pushed as revision 2. Scope = coverage top-50 (one-line default change) + closing two concrete output gaps from the pasted country US run: (1) backfill Cloudflare Radar quality data for "no coverage"/"remote-only" ISP rows that today skip _measure() and render blank, surfacing it in both the per-ISP section and summary; (2) make speedtest.run() return partial results so an upload timeout no longer discards a successful download. 6 requirements, 7 acceptance criteria, 2 phases. Workflow: bug-fix / standard (scope → implementation → review → release; design and architecture skipped). Because "cover all the gaps" is open-ended, I flagged the open question in Technical Notes — reverse-DNS hop enrichment and per-ISP throughput are deferred/out of scope pending your confirmation.

events.note: Adding Phase 3 (PeeringDB netixlan trace-target discovery) as revision 3

events.change_summary: Added Phase 3: PeeringDB netixlan trace-target discovery to fill the "no trace target" gap at its source. ASN-pair path analysis split into a separate proposed task (not in this scope).

events.summary: Revision 3 pushed. Added Phase 3 — PeeringDB netixlan trace-target discovery — reusing the PeeringDB client already in ixp.py to extend country.get_test_ip_for_asn with a non-RIPE source of reachable per-ASN targets (the ASN's IXP interface IPs). This fills the "no trace target" gap at its source; Radar backfill (Phase 2) remains the fallback for ASNs still dark. Scope is now 8 requirements, 9 acceptance criteria, 3 phases; workflow unchanged (bug-fix/standard, no design/architecture). I left existing RIPE Atlas target lookup in place and flagged the "remove RIPE entirely?" question in Technical Notes since ripping it out affects all of country mode. Separately, the ASN-to-ASN path analysis feature (hybrid BGP-via-RouteViews/bgp.tools + opportunistic live Globalping traceroute) is proposed as its own new task — it depends on this task's PeeringDB discovery.

events.rationale: Routine sync drift: HEAD fast-forwarded (stale HEAD is a strict ancestor of current) with a byte-identical dirty-status digest; swept forward automatically without waking an agent.

events.details: Full suite (146 tests) passes; ruff check src tests clean. No new tests needed for the one-line default change; coverage --help confirms default 50, title interpolates top so --top 10 yields "Top 10 Countries".

events.change_summary: Phase 1 complete: coverage default raised to 50

events.summary: Raised coverage command --top default from 20 to 50. Title already interpolates top so no title change needed. All 146 tests pass, ruff clean.

items.description: Run `netpath coverage` with no flag and confirm the table title reads `Globalping Coverage — Top 50 Countries` and lists up to 50 ranked country rows.
Run `netpath coverage --top 10` and confirm exactly 10 rows appear with a `Top 10 Countries` title (override still works).
Run `netpath coverage -t 5` and confirm the short flag also overrides to 5 rows / `Top 5 Countries`.

events.note: Phase 2: backfill Radar for uncovered/remote-only ISP rows + resilient partial baseline speedtest

events.description: Starting phase 2: fill uncovered/remote-only rows + salvage partial baselines

events.details: 146 baseline + 4 new tests/test_speedtest.py partial-baseline tests. ruff check src tests clean.

alternatives_considered: Keep run() raising and only salvage partial results in the baseline caller — rejected because it leaves the _measure() fallback still discarding a good direction and duplicates try/except logic.
events.decision: Implementation: speedtest.run() now never raises on a single-direction failure — it returns {download, upload, server, errors} with per-direction results (None on failure). extract_stats() returns (upload|None, download|None). Both the baseline caller and the _measure() speedtest fallback were updated to consume partial results, so a successful download survives an upload timeout in every path (not just the baseline).
events.rationale: Mirrors the existing probe_errors convention (per-probe error strings, best-effort partial results). Changing run() at the source rather than only patching the baseline caller means the _measure() fallback also stops discarding a good direction — a strict improvement consistent with R7 with no extra risk since _measure already tolerated a fully-failed speedtest.

events.change_summary: Phase 2 complete: Radar backfill for uncovered/remote-only rows + resilient partial baseline

events.summary: Backfilled Cloudflare Radar for remote-only/no-coverage ISP rows (per-ISP panel + summary-tree figures via _fetch_rum, no live target), and made speedtest.run() return partial results so a failed direction no longer discards the one that succeeded. Baseline panel now shows the succeeded direction and marks the failed one; both-fail preserves the warning. 150 tests pass, ruff clean.

items.description: Run `NETPATH_CF_TOKEN=<radar:read token> netpath country US` and confirm at least one formerly `remote-only` or `no coverage` ISP now prints a `Cloudflare Radar` panel in its section, and shows a `Radar: ↓ … ↑ … idle …` line under that ISP in the `US summary` tree.
Run `netpath country US` with NO `NETPATH_CF_TOKEN` set and confirm remote-only / no-coverage rows render exactly as before — bare labels, no empty Radar values or panels.

Simulate a partial baseline: on a link where the upload times out (or throttle upload), run `netpath country US` and confirm the `Your baseline · speed.cloudflare.com` panel still shows the `↓ Download` figure and marks `↑ Upload` as `failed`, instead of only printing a warning.

Confirm the both-directions-fail case still behaves as before: with the network fully blocked to `speed.cloudflare.com`, `netpath country US` prints the `Baseline speedtest failed:` warning and no baseline panel.
Run `uv run pytest -q` and confirm 150 passed, then `uv run ruff check src tests` reports `All checks passed!`.

workflow.rationale: Primarily fills incomplete/broken output behavior (blank ISP rows, discarded partial baseline) plus a one-line coverage default change. No new web UI — all output is existing Rich terminal patterns, so skip design. Well-contained to cli.py/display.py/speedtest.py building on existing patterns (reuses _fetch_rum, probe_errors convention), no new subsystems or system boundaries, so skip architecture. stageContent.implementation:
## Phase 1: Expand coverage to top 50

Raised the `coverage` command's `--top` default from 20 to 50 (`src/netpath/cli.py`). The table title already interpolates `top`, so no title change was needed.

**Files changed:** `src/netpath/cli.py` (one line).

## Phase 2: Fill uncovered/remote-only ISP data and salvage partial baselines

Closed two concrete output gaps from the pasted `country US` run.

**Resilient baseline speedtest (R7/R8):**
- `speedtest.run()` no longer raises when one direction fails. It attempts download and upload independently and returns `{"download": {...}|None, "upload": {...}|None, "server": ..., "errors": {direction: reason}}`, mirroring the `probe_errors` convention.
- `speedtest.extract_stats()` returns `(upload|None, download|None)` so callers render whatever succeeded.
- Both consumers updated: the baseline block (`cli.py`) and the `_measure()` speedtest fallback (`cli.py`). A successful download now survives an upload timeout in every path. Both-directions-fail preserves the original warning with no panel.
- `display.baseline_panel(upload, download, errors=...)` now tolerates a None direction and marks the failed one as `failed` rather than dropping the whole panel.

**Radar backfill for blank rows (R5/R6):**
- The two short-circuit branches (`remote_only`, `skip_reason`/no-coverage) now call the existing `_fetch_rum(asn_str, cf_token)` before `continue`. When Radar data returns, `display.rum_only_panel` prints it in the per-ISP section and the Radar dict is stored on the summary row under a `rum` key (matching what `_measure` sets).
- `display.country_summary` renders a compact Radar figures subrow (`_render_rum_subrow` / `_rum_summary_str`) beneath remote-only and no-coverage rows when `rum` is present. With no token, `_fetch_rum` returns None and rows render exactly as before — no empty values.

**Tests:** `tests/test_speedtest.py` — upload-fails/download-succeeds returns partial (no raise), download-fails/upload-succeeds, both-fail records both errors, both-succeed.

**Files changed:** `src/netpath/speedtest.py`, `src/netpath/cli.py`, `src/netpath/display.py`, `tests/test_speedtest.py`.

**Verification:** 150 tests pass (146 baseline + 4 new); `ruff check src tests` clean. Radar backfill (needs a live token) and both-fail panel suppression are covered in the manual test list.
